questions.question: Which pattern ensures only one instance of a class exists throughout the application?
questions.explanation: The Singleton pattern restricts instantiation to exactly one object and provides a global access point to that instance.

questions.question: The Factory Method pattern is classified as which type of design pattern?
questions.explanation: Factory Method is a Creational pattern — it defines an interface for creating objects but lets subclasses decide which class to instantiate.

questions.question: Which pattern is best for constructing a complex object step by step?
questions.explanation: The Builder pattern separates complex object construction into discrete steps, allowing different representations using the same process.
